perturbation.original_text: b) Separate and apart from the one-time sponsorship initiation fee NetGrocer shall pay to Excite sponsorship and advertising fees for the first year of the term of the Agreement in the total amount of [*] payable in nine equal monthly installments of [*], commencing on March 15, 1998. NetGrocer will pay the remainder of the monthly installments on or prior to the fifteenth day of each of the next eight (8) calendar months.
perturbation.changed_text: b) Separate and apart from the one-time sponsorship initiation fee NetGrocer shall pay to Excite sponsorship and advertising fees for the first year of the term of the Agreement in the total amount of [*] payable in nine equal monthly installments of [*], commencing on March 15, 1998. NetGrocer will pay the remainder of the monthly installments on or prior to the last day of each of the next eight (8) calendar months.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the payment deadline. Originally, payments were due on or prior to the fifteenth day of each month. Now, they are due on or prior to the last day of each month. This contradicts the original, creating uncertainty about the actual payment due date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: b) Separate and apart from the one-time sponsorship initiation fee NetGrocer shall pay to Excite sponsorship and advertising fees for the first year of the term of the Agreement in the total amount of [*] payable in nine equal monthly installments of [*], commencing on March 15, 1998. NetGrocer will pay the remainder of the monthly installments on or prior to the last day of each of the next eight (8) calendar months.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline from 'on or prior to the fifteenth day' to 'on or prior to the last day' of the month. The contradicted text maintains that same changed phrase: 'on or prior to the last day of each of the next eight (8) calendar months'. However, this changed phrase is now different from the original, causing a direct conflict with a previous clause that required payment by the fifteenth day. The change creates an inconsistency related to payment deadlines which leads to an in-text contradiction, as defined under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: c) Separate and apart from the one-time sponsorship initiation fee, NetGrocer shall pay to Excite sponsorship and advertising fees for the second year of the term of the Agreement in the total amount of [*] payable in equal monthly installments of [*], commencing on December 15, 1998. NetGrocer will pay the remainder of the monthly installments on or prior to the fifteenth day of each of the next eleven (11) calendar months.
perturbation.changed_text: c) Separate and apart from the one-time sponsorship initiation fee, NetGrocer shall pay to Excite sponsorship and advertising fees for the second year of the term of the Agreement in the total amount of [*] payable in equal monthly installments of [*], commencing on December 15, 1998. NetGrocer will pay the remainder of the monthly installments within ten (10) days of the invoice date of each of the next eleven (11) calendar months.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the payment deadline. Originally, payments were due on or prior to the fifteenth day of each month. Now, they are due within ten (10) days of the invoice date of each month. This contradicts the original, creating uncertainty about the actual payment due date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c) Separate and apart from the one-time sponsorship initiation fee, NetGrocer shall pay to Excite sponsorship and advertising fees for the second year of the term of the Agreement in the total amount of [*] payable in equal monthly installments of [*], commencing on December 15, 1998. NetGrocer will pay the remainder of the monthly installments within ten (10) days of the invoice date of each of the next eleven (11) calendar months.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline from 'on or prior to the fifteenth day of each month' to 'within ten (10) days of the invoice date of each month'. This creates a direct inconsistency and uncertainty regarding the payment due date, thus a strong in-text contradiction because now there are two different payment schedules in the same document (both the original and changed text are in the same document). This makes the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: g) If Excite misses any quarterly guaranteed Click-through amount, Excite will make good the difference within [*] days following the end of such quarter. If Excite does not make good the difference within [*] days, NetGrocer may suspend (but not eliminate) its payments of the sponsorship and advertising fees described in Section 5(b) and 5(c) until the make-good is delivered, at which time NetGrocer will resume its payments of the sponsorship and advertising fees.
perturbation.changed_text: g) If Excite misses any quarterly guaranteed Click-through amount, Excite will make good the difference within [*] days following the end of such quarter. If Excite does not make good the difference within [*] days, NetGrocer may suspend (and eliminate) its payments of the sponsorship and advertising fees described in Section 5(b) and 5(c) until the make-good is delivered, at which time NetGrocer will resume its payments of the sponsorship and advertising fees.
perturbation.explanation: The change to 'suspend (and eliminate)' directly contradicts the original 'suspend (but not eliminate)'. It introduces ambiguity regarding the extent to which payments can be suspended, making it unclear whether payments are permanently forfeited or merely temporarily paused.
perturbation.contradicted_text: g) If Excite misses any quarterly guaranteed Click-through amount, Excite will make good the difference within [*] days following the end of such quarter. If Excite does not make good the difference within [*] days, NetGrocer may suspend (and eliminate) its payments of the sponsorship and advertising fees described in Section 5(b) and 5(c) until the make-good is delivered, at which time NetGrocer will resume its payments of the sponsorship and advertising fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the clause to state NetGrocer may 'suspend (and eliminate)' payments, directly contradicting the original intention of 'suspend (but not eliminate)'. This creates a contradiction because the original text implies a temporary suspension, while the modified text suggests a permanent elimination, leading to uncertainty about the extent of payment suspension and thus in-text contradiction.
